title: Package Design, Modules, Versions, Workspaces, and Dependency Provenance · Contract Triage
summary: A team must split a command and library, publish a stable module, test a two-module workspace, and review dependency changes. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a versioned multi-package module plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Organize commands, internal packages, public packages, names, imports, cycles, and documentation around cohesive APIs.
Interpret module, go, toolchain, require, replace, exclude, retract, and godebug directives without treating local overrides as releases.
Explain semantic import versioning, minimal version selection, module graphs, sums, proxies, and private-module boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Package Api Layout
steps.instruction:
debug case — A two-module workspace proposes dependency v2.2.0, a local replace, toolchain go1.26.5, and a public v2 package path. Explain MVS, checksum and proxy evidence, workspace-only success, API naming, and release compatibility. Competency probe: Organize commands, internal packages, public packages, names, imports, cycles, and documentation around cohesive APIs.
